steps.title: Identify the median position for 11 values
steps.content: With 11 values, the median is at position $\frac{11 + 1}{2} = 6$. We need the 6th value in each dataset when listed in order.

steps.title: Find the 6th value in data set A
steps.content:
Data set A: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, 8, 11, 11, 14, 14, 14
Counting to the 6th position: 2, 2, 2, 5, 5, then 8. The median of data set A is 8.

steps.title: Find the 6th value in data set B and compare
steps.content:
Data set B: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, 8, 8, 8, 11, 11, 14
Counting to the 6th position: 2, 5, 5, 8, 8, then 8. The median of data set B is 8.
Both datasets have a median of 8. That matches choice C — C is correct.
